text: The scissor graphy reveals a vesicle vagina fistula approximately three millimeters in length. The corposcope shows a fistula opening on the left aspect of the vaginal stump, measuring about eight millimeters, with visible urine emanating from the opening.

The surgeon uses a cystoscope to visualize the fistula opening located at the trigon near the left ureteral orifice. A guide wire is visible, inserted from the vagina stump into the bladder. Due to adhesions, a cystotomy is created over the left aspect of the bladder to identify the fistula opening inside the bladder cavity. Dissection proceeds toward the posterior wall of the bladder to expose the fistula tract outside the bladder.

The surgeon extends the cystotomy edge to expose the vaginal opening of the fistula. The fibrotic tissue is removed, and the edge of the vaginal opening is trimmed. The opening is then closed using a Vigrio absorbable suture.

The surgeon closes the cystotomy wound using 3-0 Monocryl absorbable sutures. Post-surgery, the patient shows no signs of urine leakage from the vagina, and follow-up cystography confirms no recurrence of the vesicovaginal fistula.
